paper_title: Insolation and Biome Analysis in the Eurasian Subarctic

methods.description: Calculation of insolation at the top of the Earth's atmosphere in the Eurasian Subarctic for the Holocene using the La2004 orbital model, which includes major planets, Pluto, and post-Newtonian corrections.

steps.description: The calculation of insolation at the top of the Earth's atmosphere in the Eurasian Subarctic for the Holocene is carried out based on the solution La2004 for the long-period evolution of the Earth's orbit.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate insolation using La2004 orbital model

steps.description: The orbital model takes into account eight major planets of the Solar System and the dwarf planet Pluto. The post-Newtonian general relativity corrections of order 1/c 2 due to the Sun are included.
steps.searchable_summary: include major planets and post-Newtonian corrections in model

steps.description: The Moon is treated as a separate object. In order to obtain a realistic evolution of the Earth-Moon System, which also takes into account the most important coefficient (dynamical form-factor of the Sun J2S) in the gravitational potential of the Earth and of the Moon, and the tidal dissipation in the Earth-Moon System.
steps.searchable_summary: model Earth-Moon system with J2S and tidal dissipation

steps.description: The precession and obliquity equations for the Earth and the evolution of its rotation period is also integrated at the same time.

methods.method_name: Surface Topography and Solar Radiation Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of surface topography and calculation of solar radiation for the recent period and Pliocene epoch using digital elevation models and hemispherical viewshed algorithm.

steps.description: The surface topography was analyzed for the recent period (the Urals and West Siberia) and for the Pliocene epoch (West Siberia).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze surface topography for recent and Pliocene periods
inputs: DEM data for recent and Pliocene periods

steps.description: The digital elevation model (DEM) for the Pliocene (Pre-Yamalian time) was built based on a paleogeomorphological map at a scale of 1: 4,000,000.
steps.searchable_summary: build Pliocene DEM from paleogeomorphological map

steps.description: DEM of the Pliocene and the modern period were divided by parallels into segments of 1°.
For each segment, annual solar radiation was calculated (365 days for a non-leap year) at monthly intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate annual solar radiation for each DEM segment

steps.description: The sum of direct and diffuse radiation was determined using the methods of the hemispherical viewshed algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: determine direct and diffuse radiation using viewshed algorithm

steps.description: Since the cloud-cover data for the Pliocene were not available, we used constants for atmospheric transmissivity and diffuse proportion parameters for generally clear sky conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: apply constants for atmospheric transmissivity and diffuse proportion

steps.description: Further, these segments were combined into single surface insolation models for the two analyzed periods, and an estimate of the transformation of the amount of solar radiation due to changes in the topography was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: combine segments into insolation models and estimate transformation

methods.description: Calculation of biomes using pollen analysis data from peatland sections and mapping reconstructed biomes over time.

steps.description: To calculate biomes, we used the results of pollen analysis of 24 sections of peatlands in the territory of the Middle, Northern, Subpolar, Polar Urals and South Yamal.
steps.searchable_summary: use pollen analysis data from peatland sections

steps.description: For calculations, we used the initial tables of pollen spectra: the percentage of pollen and spores of each sample, where the sum of pollen from trees and shrubs was taken as 100%.
steps.searchable_summary: process pollen spectra tables for biome calculations

steps.description: All examined sections are dated to 14 С, which allowed us to build depth-age models for each section Clam 2.0.
Reconstructions were carried out according to time sections of the Holocene with an interval of 1000 years, respectively, for a time of 11,000 years ago, 10,000, 9,000, ..., 0 years ago.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct biomes for Holocene time sections

steps.description: According to the coordinates of the sections, the reconstructed biomes were plotted on the map of the corresponding time slice in the form of conditional points colored in a specific color for each biome.
steps.searchable_summary: plot reconstructed biomes on map for each time slice

steps.description: Modern biomes reconstructed from surface pollen spectrum are compared with the map 'Biomes of Russia'.
steps.searchable_summary: compare modern reconstructed biomes with Biomes of Russia map
